title: 360_Bloom linear chain + bloom onset (L4 LEAD)
goal: 360_Bloom 11.58 dB -> LEAD/worst of the S2/S4 linear-chain+colour family. FCP keeps the WHOLE filter+blend chain in kCGColorSpaceLinearSRGB and encodes to sRGB ONCE at readback; the engine encodes per-filter. Infra exists (compositor/linear.ts, flag-gated). A per-filter linear encode is a PROVEN DEAD-END (regresses stacked transitions) - build the WHOLE-CHAIN float pass, encode once, flip ON family-by-family, gate all 65 after each. Bloom-float is decoded + register-verified but its threshold TEMPORAL ONSET over-blooms (FCT_BLOOM_FLOAT ON regresses Lights_Bloom -1.0, 360_Bloom -1.1) - stays OFF until the onset curve is decoded. DoD: linear chain lands family-by-family gate-green; do NOT commit red.
notes: | MEASURED 2026-07-18: the decode-faithful FLOAT bloom (FCT_BLOOM_FLOAT path in glow.ts, now using decimatedBlurFloatRGB) scores Lights__Bloom 18.60->18.89 (+0.29) but 360_Bloom 11.52->10.74 (-0.78) = NET -0.49 on the GUI gate. So the float bloom is still NOT gate-ready: the x10 extract + additive-Brightness/50 combine energy diverges from GT on the LIVE-evaluating 360 host (Lights__Bloom retime-wraps so its bloom barely fires). The FCT_BLOOM_FLOAT flag is a Rule-12 violation (tracked T-qdeflag0001) — but the default-OFF 8-bit path is the gate-correct one until the extract/combine energy is re-decoded to match BOTH Bloom slugs' GUI GT. Blur sub-kernel is already VERIFIED (PAEGaussianBlur). NEXT: re-decode the bloomHeliumRender combine gain/threshold against 360_Bloom GT specifically (it evaluates live, no wrap confound). | SHARPENED 2026-07-18 (isolated Bloom probe): the FLOAT bloom is the FAITHFUL filter — at the 360_Bloom shipping params (Amount=32,Brightness=100,Thr=0) it matches headless 50.4 dB isolated (mean 255=full white) while the 8-bit glowFilter path is BROKEN at 3.7 dB (mean 97, barely blooms). So the filter MAGNITUDE is correct in float. The 360_Bloom GUI-gate regression is NOT magnitude but ONSET TIMING: per-frame the engine's bloom peaks too EARLY (engine f09 mean 194.9 vs GUI GT f09 118.7; both reach ~223-227 at the f14 peak). The Threshold curve (100->3 at 0.117-0.15s ->100 at 0.25s) is evaluated at a different scene-time than FCP for THIS host (360-aware retime path) = the BLOCKER-A retime-wrap coupling. NEXT: fix the Bloom Threshold curve-time mapping for the 360/retime host so the float bloom onset aligns; then ship float bloom UNCONDITIONALLY (removes the Rule-12 FCT_BLOOM_FLOAT flag) — it is the correct filter, only the host timing gates it. Blur core already VERIFIED.
